behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff boundary (three source clients -> aggregation/orchestration -> summary step input). A unit test of one function can miss wiring omissions. Real OS/network/LLM output is not required to catch this specific failure; in-process stubs for the three sources and a spy/fake summary step are sufficient. Rejected higher levels: behavior text only requires inclusion of source data in handoff payload, not real HTTP behavior or model-quality judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real collection/orchestration modules with stubbed source adapters returning distinct markers per source, run one report build, and assert the exact payload sent to summary-generation contains all three markers/sections.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback formatting: source adapter failure must be translated into section-level placeholder while the pipeline continues. A pure unit test may miss orchestration behavior. Rejected System: behavior does not require real network timeout semantics, only unavailable-source handling, which a failing stub can reproduce. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: correctness is fallback control flow, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source stubs returning data and one source stub raising an availability error; assert run completes, output includes `data unavailable` for only that section, and other sections are still populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about summary content fidelity, which depends on real model output quality/judgment. A mock or deterministic fixture can validate wrapper logic but cannot reveal hallucination, omission, or misstatement. Rejected Integration: text requires accuracy of generated narrative itself, not only routing/retries/payload shape. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic decision point (summary generation) is required, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against the real model on a fixed dataset with known key values and assert factual coverage/consistency via a rubric (e.g., required values present, no contradictory numbers), allowing for wording variance.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is external delivery infrastructure: SMTP/API connectivity, provider acceptance, and inbox receipt. In-process fakes can hide real delivery failures. Rejected Agentic: although the pipeline may include summary generation, this behavior’s pass criterion is end-to-end delivery to inbox, not model output quality. Rejected Workflow: no required sequence of multiple agentic decisions is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full script against reachable test source endpoints and a real test email account/mailbox; verify one run sends a report email that arrives in the configured inbox with expected subject/body structure.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is state handoff/persistence across runs: failed send -> file fallback -> subsequent run reloads and retries. This can be caught by wiring modules with a failing email adapter then a succeeding adapter across two invocations, using real local filesystem in-process. Integration-brake checks: (a) no mandatory real OS/network behavior beyond controlled adapter failure; (b) no dependence on model output quality; (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System: behavior text does not require validating actual scheduler/cron or real mail provider outages, only retry semantics across scheduled runs.
behaviors.test_description: Invocation 1: email adapter returns unreachable error; assert report file is written. Invocation 2 (simulated next run): email adapter succeeds; assert persisted report is retried/sent and fallback file state is cleared/updated appropriately.
